title: feat: tag opposite prompt images
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Publishes successfully delivered bot images with `opposite-prompt-bot`
- Keeps media publishing failures from breaking Discord replies
- Uploads after reply delivery to avoid retry duplicates

Checks: Biome, esbuild, source/output tagging contract

gist.headline: The bot leaves a pollen trail
gist.blurb: Opposite Prompt Bot now tags the images it successfully delivers before sending them out into the Discord wild. Publishing happens after the reply, so a fussy upload cannot interrupt the conversation.
gist.summary: Opposite Prompt Bot now publishes successfully delivered images with the `opposite-prompt-bot` source tag after its Discord reply is sent, while isolating media-upload failures from reply delivery.
gist.impact: Discord users keep receiving bot images reliably, and images that arrive successfully become discoverable as creations from Opposite Prompt Bot without retry duplicates.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy Discord-adjacent bot delivery scene: Polly acts as a careful post bee, attaching a tiny `opposite-prompt-bot` tag to a finished pixel image after a friendly robot has delivered it through a glowing chat terminal. A small code-plant and garden labels suggest that completed creations are now planted into the wider gallery, while a harmless upload-error paper airplane drifts away without disturbing the delivered message.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush, light blue wings, and a tiny messenger hat, carefully tying a pixel-font tag reading "opposite-prompt-bot" onto a finished artwork beside a warm glowing Discord-style retro terminal; a cheerful CRT monitor robot has already delivered the image, while code-plants bloom from lime green (#ecf874) pots. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic but beautiful.
